**Daniel Robert Snow** (born 3 December 1978) is a British-Canadian popular historian and television presenter. He is the founder of [History Hit](https://www.historyhit.com/), a global History media network. He is an ambassador of the [Electoral Reform Society](/wiki/Electoral_Reform_Society "Electoral Reform Society") (ERS).

## Early life and education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dan_Snow&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life and education")]

Dan Robert Snow was born on 3 December 1978 in [Westminster](/wiki/Westminster "Westminster"), London.[[1]](#cite_note-1) He is the youngest son of [Peter Snow](/wiki/Peter_Snow "Peter Snow"), [BBC](/wiki/BBC "BBC") [television journalist](/wiki/Television_journalist "Television journalist"), and Ann MacMillan, a Canadian and managing editor [emerita](/wiki/Emeritus "Emeritus") of [CBC](/wiki/Canadian_Broadcasting_Corporation "Canadian Broadcasting Corporation")'s London Bureau; thus he holds dual British and Canadian nationality.[[2]](#cite_note-2) Through his mother, he is the nephew of Canadian historian [Margaret MacMillan](/wiki/Margaret_MacMillan "Margaret MacMillan") and also a great-great-grandson of British Prime Minister [David Lloyd George](/wiki/David_Lloyd_George "David Lloyd George").[[3]](#cite_note-historyboy-3)

Snow was educated in London at [Westfield Primary School](/wiki/Barnes_Primary_School "Barnes Primary School") (now [Barnes Primary School](/wiki/Barnes_Primary_School "Barnes Primary School")) and at [St Paul's School](/wiki/St_Paul%27s_School_(London) "St Paul's School (London)") where he was [Captain of School](/wiki/School_Captain "School Captain") and [rowed](/wiki/Rowing_(sport) "Rowing (sport)") for its [VIII](/wiki/Eight_(rowing) "Eight (rowing)"). He then went to [Balliol College, Oxford](/wiki/Balliol_College,_Oxford "Balliol College, Oxford"),[[4]](#cite_note-4) his father's alma mater, and graduated with [first-class honours](/wiki/First-class_honours "First-class honours") in Modern History.[[3]](#cite_note-historyboy-3)[[5]](#cite_note-historian-5) A keen rower since his secondary school days, he won the U-23 men's division at the 2000 [British Indoor Rowing Championships](/wiki/British_Indoor_Rowing_Championships "British Indoor Rowing Championships")[[6]](#cite_note-6) and rowed three times in [the Boat Race](/wiki/The_Boat_Race "The Boat Race"), winning in [2000](/wiki/The_Boat_Race_2000 "The Boat Race 2000") and losing the controversial [2001](/wiki/The_Boat_Race_2001 "The Boat Race 2001") Boat Race when club President.[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dan_Snow&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

Snow presented his first programme in October 2002 just after graduating from university, co-presenting the BBC's 60th anniversary special on the [Battles of El Alamein](/wiki/Second_Battle_of_El_Alamein "Second Battle of El Alamein") with his father [Peter](/wiki/Peter_Snow "Peter Snow").[[9]](#cite_note-el_alamein-9) The two then collaborated to present an eight-part documentary series called *[Battlefield Britain](/wiki/Battlefield_Britain "Battlefield Britain")*, which aired in 2004, and won a [BAFTA Craft Award](/wiki/BAFTA_Craft_Award "BAFTA Craft Award") for [special effects](/wiki/Special_effect "Special effect"). The same year, Snow won a [Sony award](/wiki/Radio_Academy_Awards "Radio Academy Awards") as one of the presenters covering the Oxford-Cambridge [Boat Race](/wiki/The_Boat_Race "The Boat Race") on the Thames.

Snow has presented on many [state occasions](/wiki/State_occasion "State occasion") such as the 200th anniversary celebration of the [Battle of Trafalgar](/wiki/Battle_of_Trafalgar "Battle of Trafalgar"), *[Beating Retreat](/wiki/Beating_Retreat "Beating Retreat")* 2006, the 60th anniversary of the end of World War Two, the 90th anniversary of the World War I [Armistice](/wiki/Armistice "Armistice") in November 2008, [Trooping the Colour](/wiki/Trooping_the_Colour "Trooping the Colour") and the [Lord Mayor's Show](/wiki/Lord_Mayor%27s_Show "Lord Mayor's Show"). Snow again collaborated with his father to present [BBC 2](/wiki/BBC_2 "BBC 2")'s *[20th Century Battlefields](/wiki/20th_Century_Battlefields "20th Century Battlefields")* and its print edition.[[10]](#cite_note-10) The series covers battles all around the world and is presented in similar fashion to the first *Battlefield Britain*.

In 2015 he founded [History Hit](https://www.historyhit.com/sign-up/?campaignid=21246459515&adgroupid=159024365022&adid=788778137923&utm_term=history%20hit&utm_campaign=OD+%7C+Search+%7C+Brand+%7C+Australia+%26+NZ&utm_source=adwords&utm_medium=ppc&hsa_acc=7076869466&hsa_cam=21246459515&hsa_grp=159024365022&hsa_ad=788778137923&hsa_src=g&hsa_tgt=kwd-334971619652&hsa_kw=history%20hit&hsa_mt=e&hsa_net=adwords&hsa_ver=3&gad_source=1&gad_campaignid=21246459515&gbraid=0AAAAADLkojBTz_yNGuF1DWR4To-FwCYqS&gclid=CjwKCAiAqprNBhB6EiwAMe3yhnCEE3xyVVO1RTENMSWtzkN6oeHKVAdgtYhyf3QTwayIqXIsf1KCixoCyPoQAvD_BwE), his own History media brand. Starting as a podcast, this has grown into a popular network encompassing a streaming service, Youtube channel and multiple podcasts.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

Snow presented on the Canadian Broadcasting Corporation's specials, with his mother [Ann MacMillan](/w/index.php?title=Ann_MacMillan&action=edit&redlink=1 "Ann MacMillan (page does not exist)"), for the [funeral of Queen Elizabeth](/wiki/Death_and_state_funeral_of_Elizabeth_II "Death and state funeral of Elizabeth II") in 2022 and the [coronation of Charles III and Camilla](/wiki/Coronation_of_Charles_III_and_Camilla "Coronation of Charles III and Camilla") the following year.

In early 2022, Snow was part of the Endurance22 expedition that found [Antarctic](/wiki/Antarctic "Antarctic") explorer [Sir Ernest Shackleton](/wiki/Sir_Ernest_Shackleton "Sir Ernest Shackleton")'s lost vessel, *[Endurance](/wiki/Endurance_(1912_ship) "Endurance (1912 ship)")*, 107 years after it sank in the [Weddell Sea](/wiki/Weddell_Sea "Weddell Sea").[[12]](#cite_note-12) He danced with [Nadiya Bychkova](/wiki/Nadiya_Bychkova "Nadiya Bychkova") for the [2023 *Strictly Come Dancing Christmas Special*](/wiki/List_of_Strictly_Come_Dancing_specials#2023 "List of Strictly Come Dancing specials").

### Awards and honours

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dan_Snow&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Awards and honours")]

Snow was appointed [Member of the Order of the British Empire](/wiki/Member_of_the_Order_of_the_British_Empire "Member of the Order of the British Empire") (MBE) in the [2019 Birthday Honours](/wiki/2019_Birthday_Honours "2019 Birthday Honours") for services to history.[[54]](#cite_note-54) In 2019 Snow was awarded a [Doctor of Letters (DLitt)](/wiki/Doctor_of_Letters "Doctor of Letters") honoris causa from [Lancaster University](/wiki/Lancaster_University "Lancaster University").[[55]](#cite_note-55) Other awards and honours include:

* BAFTA (Visual Effects) for 'Battlefield Britain'
* Sony Award (Best Live Coverage) for Boat Race Day
* BAFTA Cymru (Best Presenter) for 'Hadrian'
* Maritime Media Award for best television, film or radio for 'Empire of the Seas'
* 2011 History Makers Award (Most Innovative Production) for 'Battle for North America' a 1-hour special on Snow's book 'Death or Victory.' Produced by Snow's production company Ballista
* [Voice of the Listener & Viewer](/wiki/Voice_of_the_Listener_%26_Viewer "Voice of the Listener & Viewer") Special Award 2013

On 27 November 2010, Snow married the [criminologist](/wiki/Criminologist "Criminologist") and philanthropist [Lady Edwina Louise Grosvenor](/wiki/Lady_Edwina_Louise_Grosvenor "Lady Edwina Louise Grosvenor"),[[56]](#cite_note-56) second daughter of [The 6th Duke of Westminster](/wiki/Gerald_Grosvenor,_6th_Duke_of_Westminster "Gerald Grosvenor, 6th Duke of Westminster").[[57]](#cite_note-57) The couple were married by the [Bishop of Liverpool](/wiki/Bishop_of_Liverpool "Bishop of Liverpool"), [James Jones](/wiki/James_Jones_(bishop) "James Jones (bishop)"), at [his official residence, Bishop's Lodge](/wiki/Bishop%27s_palace "Bishop's palace").[[58]](#cite_note-58) The couple have three children, the eldest and youngest being daughters. Their home, in the [New Forest](/wiki/New_Forest "New Forest"), has a private beach.[[59]](#cite_note-59)

On 18 April 2010, Snow and a few friends took three [rigid-hulled inflatable boats](/wiki/Rigid-hulled_inflatable_boat "Rigid-hulled inflatable boat") from [Dover](/wiki/Dover "Dover") to [Calais](/wiki/Calais "Calais") to help 25 people return to Britain, after they had been stranded in France by the [air travel disruption after the Icelandic eruption](/wiki/Air_travel_disruption_after_the_2010_Eyjafjallaj%C3%B6kull_eruption "Air travel disruption after the 2010 Eyjafjallajökull eruption"). At Calais they were told by the French authorities that they could not return to collect any more.[[60]](#cite_note-60)

In August 2011, he chased [a group of rioters](/wiki/2011_England_riots "2011 England riots") through [Notting Hill](/wiki/Notting_Hill "Notting Hill") in west London before tackling and performing a [citizen's arrest](/wiki/Citizen%27s_arrest "Citizen's arrest") on a looter who was fleeing from a shoe shop.[[61]](#cite_note-61)[[62]](#cite_note-62)

Snow serves as president of the [Council for British Archaeology](/wiki/Council_for_British_Archaeology "Council for British Archaeology") and is a member of the [Royal Historical Society](/wiki/Royal_Historical_Society "Royal Historical Society").[[63]](#cite_note-63) As an [atheist](/wiki/Atheist "Atheist") and a [humanist](/wiki/Secular_humanism "Secular humanism"), he is a patron of [Humanists UK](/wiki/Humanists_UK "Humanists UK")[[64]](#cite_note-64) and an Honorary Associate of the [National Secular Society](/wiki/National_Secular_Society "National Secular Society").[[65]](#cite_note-65) He is also an advocate for [political reform](/wiki/Electoral_reform#United_Kingdom "Electoral reform"), being the [Electoral Reform Society](/wiki/Electoral_Reform_Society "Electoral Reform Society")'s first ambassador. He played a prominent part in the [2011 Alternative Vote referendum in the UK](/wiki/2011_United_Kingdom_Alternative_Vote_referendum "2011 United Kingdom Alternative Vote referendum"); after he released a viral video, the campaign used a version of it, featuring him, as their final referendum broadcast.

In August 2014, Snow was one of [200 public figures who were signatories to a letter](/wiki/List_of_endorsements_in_the_2014_Scottish_independence_referendum "List of endorsements in the 2014 Scottish independence referendum") to *[The Guardian](/wiki/The_Guardian "The Guardian")* expressing their hope that Scotland would vote to remain part of the United Kingdom in September's [referendum on that issue](/wiki/2014_Scottish_independence_referendum "2014 Scottish independence referendum").[[66]](#cite_note-66) In June 2019, Snow wrote in a [Twitter](/wiki/Twitter "Twitter") thread [[67]](#cite_note-67) if [Brexit](/wiki/Brexit "Brexit") happened and if the [Scottish National Party](/wiki/Scottish_National_Party "Scottish National Party") won a majority of votes in Scotland, he would "get" why Scottish people would want to have a second referendum, as leaving the European Union could "put up barriers" for Scotland. He was then asked if this meant he now advise Scots to vote for independence, and he replied "No way. One thing Brexit has taught me is the utter insanity of trying to rip countries apart".[[68]](#cite_note-68)

Snow was one of sixteen board members of [More United](/wiki/More_United "More United"), which endorsed candidates in parliamentary elections that support their values. The movement was set-up in July 2016 "to stand up for our values of opportunity, tolerance, the environment, democracy, and openness".[[69]](#cite_note-69)

Snow is an Honorary Captain in the [Royal Naval Reserve](/wiki/Royal_Naval_Reserve "Royal Naval Reserve").[[70]](#cite_note-70)
